summary: Strong, well-scoped contract proposal. 22 criteria comprehensively cover Sprint 6's run-lifecycle theme (F6 + F14): permadeath state machine, run summary, single-slot save/load with anti-save-scumming, seed reproducibility, and daily-seed runs. Criteria are concrete, testable, and bounded. Regression coverage of Sprints 1-5 is included. The proposal explicitly acknowledges out-of-scope items (chronicle markdown, multi-slot, encrypted saves) so Sprint 6 won't sprawl. Accepting with a few advisory notes the generator should heed; none rise to a blocking concern.

coverageAnalysis.F6_permadeath_run_lifecycle: Covered by C2 (game_over inert), C3 (cause_of_death), C4 (counters), C5 (run_summary), C6 (CLI prints summary on death), C17 (death exit signal vs clean quit), C22 (deepest_floor monotonicity). Complete.
coverageAnalysis.F14_seeded_and_daily_runs: Covered by C13 (daily_seed determinism), C14 (--daily CLI), C15 (end-to-end --seed reproducibility across processes). Complete.
coverageAnalysis.save_load_subtheme: Covered by C7 (save format keys), C8 (load equivalence), C9 (single-slot overwrite), C10 (anti-save-scumming consume-on-load + refuse-on-death), C11 (corrupt-save handling), C12 (CLI --save/--load), C16 (save round-trip determinism), C21 (schema versioning). Complete and notably defensive.
coverageAnalysis.regression: C18 enumerates Sprint 1-5 regression checks; regressionSprints field also lists [1,2,3,4,5]. Aligned with handoff regression registry expectations.

strengths: Every criterion has explicit, executable testSteps -- no vague 'works correctly' criteria.
Anti-save-scumming (C10) is operationalized as observable behavior (file deletion + save refusal) rather than a hand-wavy guarantee.

C11 (corrupt-save handling) names three distinct failure modes (missing, malformed JSON, schema mismatch) and requires a single documented exception class -- prevents the generator from leaking raw stdlib exceptions.
C16 (save round-trip determinism) is the right invariant: save+load+replay must equal continue-without-saving. This catches subtle RNG-state bugs that simpler equivalence tests miss.
C21 (schema version) future-proofs the format -- a small but important detail.
C6 explicitly delegates 'how to deterministically simulate death in headless mode' to the implementer, which avoids over-prescribing the API while keeping the criterion testable.
Out-of-scope list is precise and references downstream sprints (7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12) so scope creep is prevented.

concerns.issue: C12 step 3 verifies the 'rendered initial frame matches the loaded state' via 'same seed appears in stdout or the same dungeon hash'. This is loose. Suggest the generator print the master seed as part of every headless frame banner so the test is unambiguous (this is also what C14 step 4 implies).

The criterion requires try_move/descend/ascend to return False post-death, but Sprint 1-5 may not have established a return-value convention for these methods. The generator should verify existing call sites in cli.py/game.py don't break if these methods now return False on game_over.

C17 says 'Quitting saves the run by default (resumable)', but C12 ties save behavior to the --save PATH flag. The contract should clarify whether a quit without --save also persists, or whether --save is required. Suggest the generator document the rule explicitly: save-on-quit happens IFF --save was provided, otherwise quit is non-resumable. The criterion as written can pass either way as long as the integration test uses --save tmp2.json (which it does).

Consume-on-load is unusual UX: a player who loads, plays one turn, and then the process crashes loses their save entirely. This is the contract's intent (anti-scumming) and aligns with the sprint summary, but the generator should make this behavior loud in --load output ('save consumed; surviving run will require --save on next clean quit') so users aren't surprised. Consider adding to README per C20.

The criterion requires >=10 NEW Sprint-6 tests covering 9 enumerated scenarios. This is fine, but 'distinct test functions' should be enforced -- the generator could pad with parameterized cases. Suggest the evaluator counts functions named test_* in sprint-06 test files, not pytest collected items.

'Byte-identical' frames across processes is strict; any timestamp, PID, or path-dependent string in the banner will break it. The generator should ensure the headless banner contains ONLY deterministic content (seed, dimensions, frame). This is the right invariant; just flagging it as a likely first-attempt failure mode.

acceptanceRationale: Criteria are specific, testable, and collectively cover both features (F6 permadeath/save-load and F14 seeded/daily runs) plus the regression surface. The advisory concerns are about implementation hygiene the generator can address inline; none require contract revision. Proceed to implementation.
